topic: In China employee termination: hybrid cost-risk matrix and myth-busting checklist for payroll decisions
primary_keyword: China employee termination payroll cost risk matrix

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Core framework for termination and compensation exposure.
Supports evidence-backed and process-consistent termination handling.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Supports obligations around termination-month closure and payroll-linked statutory processing.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Practical context for component-level final-pay treatment assumptions.
Supports documentation discipline in payroll treatment notes.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Emphasizes process and documentation quality as key controls for multinationals.
Supports escalation-based governance for complex cases.

consensus: Termination payout scope should follow risk and control completeness, not urgency.
Myth-driven decisions often create avoidable dispute and correction costs.
Hybrid matrix + checklist governance improves operational consistency.
uncertainties: City-level evidence thresholds and handling practices vary by case facts.
Some treatment details require local confirmation before release.
English-language summaries may not include all Chinese-language implementation nuances.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Apply impact-controllability thresholds and myth-busting checks before approving in-cycle termination payments.
